explanation:
**Kitchen measurements** follow international standards (metric system + regional culinary traditions). Recipes typically use cups, tablespoons, and grams depending on origin. Knowing equivalences is key.

## How It's Calculated

Raw rice per person: 60-80g as a side dish, 100g for a main course. This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unreasonable values.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a practical reference. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a specialized professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.a: Use 60-80g of raw rice per person as a side dish, or 100g for a main course.

faq.q: What's the difference between side dish and main course portions?
faq.a: A side dish portion is smaller (60-80g raw), while a main course needs more (100g raw) since rice expands about 3 times when cooked.

faq.q: Does this work for all types of rice?
faq.a: Yes, these ratios apply to white rice, brown rice, jasmine, basmati, and most common varieties. Cooking time may vary slightly.

faq.q: Are these measurements metric or imperial?
faq.a: This calculator uses grams (metric). For rough imperial conversion: 60g ≈ 2oz, 100g ≈ 3.5oz.
Results are practical estimates with ±5-10% variance depending on rice type, water content, and cooking method.
Yes, all our calculators are completely free and require no registration.

faq.q: Do you save or share my data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We never send your data to any server.

faq.q: Can I scale this for meal prep?
faq.a: Absolutely. Simply multiply the per-person amount by your total number of people to get the total raw rice needed.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology
